design_philosophy.core_loop: High-speed racing with dynamic hazard avoidance, energy management, and position-based progression across multiple laps
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Maintaining vehicle durability while navigating hazardous track elements and competing opponents at escalating speeds, with disqualification threat for poor placement each lap
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a customized hovercar as a wealthy elite competitor in futuristic Grand Prix racing across exotic alien worlds
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unforgiving lap-by-lap disqualification system that forces consistent placement rather than allowing dramatic comebacks; player skill determines vehicle survival through collision management rather than rubber-banding AI; Mode 7 pseudo-3D perspective is core to identity, not optional polish

difficulty.lives_system: Spare machine system grants additional race attempts based on accumulated points; losing all machines ends current attempt
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-lap checkpoints with mandatory placement requirements; failure to maintain position each lap results in disqualification
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via difficulty selection (beginner/standard/expert/master across three leagues of increasing challenge); vehicle choice affects performance characteristics; player controls boost usage timing and frequency (limited to three stored boosts)

progression.upgrade_system: Vehicle selection system with four hovercar options, each with distinct performance profiles (Captain Falcon, Dr. Stewart, Pico, Samurai Goroh); no mid-game upgrades
progression.unlock_mechanism: Three leagues (Knight, Queen, King) ordered by increasing difficulty; completion of lower leagues required to access higher difficulty tiers
replayability_hooks: Four difficulty levels per league create 12 distinct difficulty tiers
15 tracks with multiple iterations (Death Wind, Port Town, Red Canyon feature alternate pathways; Mute City has three day/night variants with different configurations)
Four character/vehicle combinations encourage replays with different handling profiles
Practice mode allows skill refinement on subset of courses

world_building_techniques: Setting exposition: Year 2560, multi-billionaire racing elite, intergalactic expansion after alien contact
Manual narrative: Eight-page comic included in SNES manual featuring Captain Falcon bounty hunting mission
Track theming: Futuristic locations (Mute City in day/evening/night variants) suggest persistent world with daily cycles
Character roster: Four iconic racers with distinct visual identities suggesting competitive personalities and rivalries

faction_indicators: Four selectable racers suggest distinct competitors with individual rivalries in racing circuit
Multi-billionaire elite class identity as player faction
danger_signals: Hazard-laden track design (slip zones, magnetic fields, mines) creates environmental threat
Opponent AI racers pose direct collision risk
Disqualification mechanic signals institutional pressure and competitive stakes
environmental_storytelling: Futuristic hovercar technology and track design suggests advanced civilization
Exotic track locations (Port Town, Red Canyon, Mute City) hint at sprawling intergalactic racing venues
Track hazards (mines, magnets, slip zones) reflect militaristic or industrial world
